title: narcissus assoanus subsp. baeticus
intro: Narcissus assoanus subsp. baeticus, a rare and endemic subspecies of the Narcissus pseudonarcissus, is a perennial plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family, commonly found in the Iberian Peninsula. Its bulbs contain a range of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and inflammation, due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of Narcissus assoanus subsp. baeticus can be found in the form of decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems.
